name: The Lion and the pond of Lacanau - loop in the Regional Natural Park Médoc
name_original: Le Lion et l'étang de Lacanau - boucle dans le Parc naturel régional Médoc
region_intro: Touring cycling around Lacanau is characterized by its diverse landscapes, including the Atlantic Ocean coastline, the serene Lac de Lacanau, and extensive pine forests. This region offers varied scenery, from fragrant pine groves to picturesque lake shores and coastal dunes. The well-maintained network of cycle paths provides numerous opportunities for rides away from traffic, allowing for immersion in nature. Best touring cycling routes around Lacanau The most popular touring cycling route is The Lion and the pond of Lacanau - loop in the Regional Natural Park Médoc, a 25.5 miles (41.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 20 minutes to complete. This route leads through the Regional Natural Park Médoc. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Moutchic Beach – Super Sud Beach, Lacanau loop from Lacanau-Océan, an easy 15.4 miles (24.7 km) path. This route connects coastal areas, offering views of the Atlantic. Local touring cyclists also love the Forest Path – Super Sud Beach, Lacanau loop from Parking du Renard, a 38.2 miles (61.4 km) trail leading through the extensive pine forests, often completed in about 3 hours 27 minutes. Touring cycling around Lacanau is defined by pine forests, lake shores, and coastal dunes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy lakeside paths to moderate forest routes. The routes in Lacanau are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 2100 reviews. More than 15000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Lacanau's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Lacanau?
A: Lacanau offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 120 options available. These routes cater to various preferences, ranging from easy lakeside paths to more challenging forest trails.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Lacanau?
A: The routes in Lacanau vary in difficulty. You'll find a significant number of easy routes, with over 95 options perfect for a relaxed ride. There are also around 20 moderate routes and a few more challenging ones for experienced cyclists. The terrain generally includes flat paths through pine forests, along lake shores, and some gentle undulations near coastal dunes.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Lacanau?
A: Yes, Lacanau is very family-friendly for cycling. Many routes are easy and mostly paved, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39335200" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lacanau Lake Cycle Path – Carrefour de la Rousse loop from Carreyre&lt;/a&gt;, which offers exceptional views and calm waters for other activities. The extensive network of cycle paths is suitable for all ages and abilities.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Lacanau?
A: Absolutely. Many of the touring cycling routes around Lacanau are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961497901/the-lion-and-the-pond-of-lacanau-loop-in-the-regional-natural-park-medoc" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Lion and the pond of Lacanau - loop in the Regional Natural Park Médoc&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through pine forests, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37620482" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moutchic Beach – Super Sud Beach, Lacanau loop from Lacanau-Océan&lt;/a&gt;, connecting coastal areas.

Q: What kind of scenic views and landmarks can I expect on touring cycling routes around Lacanau?
A: Lacanau's touring cycling routes offer diverse scenery. You can ride through fragrant pine forests, along the serene Lac de Lacanau, and past coastal dunes with glimpses of the Atlantic Ocean. Notable attractions include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/965820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moutchic Beach&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/507835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maubuisson Beach, Hourtin Lake&lt;/a&gt;, and the unique ecosystem of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/507829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cousseau Pond Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4620641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway Between Lake Lacanau and Lake Hourtin&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Lacanau?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 2100 reviews. Cyclists often praise the extensive and well-maintained network of cycle paths, the varied landscapes encompassing forests, lakes, and the ocean, and the opportunity for peaceful rides away from traffic.

Q: Are there any routes that take me through the famous pine forests?
A: Yes, Lacanau is renowned for its vast pine forests, and many routes immerse you in this unique environment. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39323561" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest Path – Super Sud Beach, Lacanau loop from Parking du Renard&lt;/a&gt;, which guides you through the heart of these extensive woodlands, offering a refreshing and scenic ride.

Q: Is it possible to cycle along the coast or near the Atlantic Ocean?
A: While Lacanau Océan is known for surfing, its cycle paths do offer 'great escapades' through the dunes and pine forest near the ocean. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37620482" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moutchic Beach – Super Sud Beach, Lacanau loop from Lacanau-Océan&lt;/a&gt; connect coastal areas, providing views of the Atlantic and the opportunity to experience the coastal landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Lacanau?
A: Lacanau is enjoyable for touring cycling across multiple seasons. The extensive pine forests offer shade during warmer months, while the lake and coastal paths provide refreshing breezes. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds, making them ideal for exploring the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there amenities like cafes or accommodation along the cycling routes?
A: Lacanau is a lively destination, and you'll find various amenities available. The areas around Lac de Lacanau, including places like Moutchic Beach and Maubuisson Beach, offer family-friendly beaches with picnic areas and often have cafes or restaurants nearby. The general atmosphere supports a 'gentle way of life,' making it welcoming for outdoor enthusiasts seeking refreshments or accommodation.

Q: Can I access Lacanau's cycling routes using public transport?
A: While specific public transport details for route access aren't provided, Lacanau is well-integrated into regional transport networks. For long-distance cycling, the legendary Vélodyssée passes through Lacanau, which often has connections to public transport hubs. It's advisable to check local transport schedules for connections to specific starting points.

Q: Are there any long-distance cycling routes that pass through Lacanau?
A: Yes, Lacanau is a key point on the legendary &lt;a href="https://en.francevelotourisme.com/cycle-route/the-tour-de-gironde-by-bike/andernos-lacanau" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vélodyssée&lt;/a&gt;, a long-distance cycling route that offers magnificent itineraries. This allows cyclists to connect Lacanau with other regions and enjoy extended tours.
tips: Good surface, built a bit narrow especially at the roundabout.
The canal des étangs, which passes through the Lacanau and Porge ponds, connects Lac d'Hourtin to the Bassin d'Arcachon. Built in the 19th century, it follows a path that was created naturally.
Possible starting point for the route of the Lion's Loop. 18km of cycle paths in the middle of the forest, accessible to all, especially children's carts.
Huge and very pretty, Lacanau beach is supervised in different places and has places to practice surfing.
